title: swe:performance-hunt Synthetic Review Dataset
description: Synthetic seed review items for swe:performance-hunt. Use these for UI smoke tests and rubric walk-throughs only.

items.title: Rank bottlenecks on a scoped flow using strong evidence (likely pass)
items.userRequest: Find the main performance bottlenecks in this repo and give me the smallest high-value fixes to try first.

notes: The repository contains one or more performance-sensitive flows with code and supporting artifacts.
Profiler output, benchmarks, traces, query plans, or bundle analysis may be available for at least part of the scoped surface.

sharedCriteria.title: Concrete Evidence
sharedCriteria.description: Claims are grounded in visible artifacts such as files, commits, CI output, metrics, or commands.
sharedCriteria.passCondition: Important claims cite specific evidence from the available sources.
sharedCriteria.failCondition: The response relies on generic advice or unsupported assertions.

sharedCriteria.title: Actionable Output
sharedCriteria.description: The response leaves the user with clear next steps, a usable report, or a concrete decision.
sharedCriteria.passCondition: The output is directly usable for follow-on work or review.
sharedCriteria.failCondition: The output is vague, purely descriptive, or does not help the next decision.

sharedCriteria.title: Instruction Adherence
sharedCriteria.description: The response follows the skill's hard constraints and workflow guidance.
sharedCriteria.passCondition: It respects the skill's do-not-use rules, evidence bar, and requested output shape.
sharedCriteria.failCondition: It violates explicit skill rules or skips required parts of the workflow.

sharedCriteria.title: Reviewable Structure
sharedCriteria.description: The output is organized so a reviewer can quickly inspect evidence, findings, and recommendations.
sharedCriteria.passCondition: The structure makes it easy to review decisions and trace them back to evidence.
sharedCriteria.failCondition: The response is hard to audit because evidence, findings, and recommendations are mixed together.

reviewQuestions: Does the response identify the specific flow or surface being optimized and the metric that matters?
Are the bottlenecks tied to concrete performance evidence or clearly labeled repo-grounded inference?
Would the proposed next steps be small enough to validate quickly?

passConditions: Names the specific surface and target metric under review.
Uses profiler data, benchmarks, query plans, traces, bundle analysis, or concrete repo evidence to support each bottleneck.
Separates strong or moderate bottlenecks from weak suspicions that still need measurement.
Recommends only small, validation-tight experiments or fixes.
States missing runtime evidence and unknowns explicitly.
failConditions: Turns the request into generic cleanup or architecture advice.
Invents bottlenecks with no measured or repo-grounded support.
Treats every suspicion as an optimization task.
Proposes broad rewrites instead of narrow next steps.
Hides the fact that runtime or profiler evidence was unavailable.
commonFailureModes: Optimizing code that is not on the scoped critical path.
Repeating performance folklore without tying it to evidence.
Forgetting to name the validation path for the proposed next step.
Expanding into observability audits or incident response instead of bottleneck hunting.

candidateOutput.content:
## Scope
- User request: Find the main performance bottlenecks in this repo and give me the smallest high-value fixes to try first.
- Primary boundary: The repository contains one or more performance-sensitive flows with code and supporting artifacts.
- Evidence reviewed: `Application code`, `Benchmark or profiler artifacts`, `Performance-related configuration`

## Draft swe:performance-hunt Output
1. Scoped surface and metric: ground this in Application code and keep it bounded to the requested surface.
2. Ranked bottlenecks: ground this in Benchmark or profiler artifacts and keep it bounded to the requested surface.
3. Evidence-backed next steps: ground this in Performance-related configuration and keep it bounded to the requested surface.
4. Weak-signal section: ground this in Operational notes and keep it bounded to the requested surface.

## Unknowns Or Residual Risk
- Profiler output, benchmarks, traces, query plans, or bundle analysis may be available for at least part of the scoped surface.

## Recommended Next Step
- Use this draft as a reviewable starting point, then run only the narrow validation or follow-up work implied by the scoped output.

candidateOutput.content:
## Scoped Readout
- User request: Find the main performance bottlenecks in this repo and give me the smallest high-value fixes to try first.
- Reviewed first: `Application code`, `Benchmark or profiler artifacts`, `Performance-related configuration`, `Operational notes`

## swe:performance-hunt Draft
1. Scoped surface and metric: tie this to Application code, then stop at the requested boundary.
2. Ranked bottlenecks: tie this to Benchmark or profiler artifacts, then stop at the requested boundary.
3. Evidence-backed next steps: tie this to Performance-related configuration, then stop at the requested boundary.
4. Weak-signal section: tie this to Operational notes, then stop at the requested boundary.

## Labeled Unknowns
- The repository contains one or more performance-sensitive flows with code and supporting artifacts.
- Profiler output, benchmarks, traces, query plans, or bundle analysis may be available for at least part of the scoped surface.

## Smallest Next Step
- Use this draft as the narrowest reviewable starting point, then validate only the touched surface.

candidateOutput.content:
## Scope
- User request: Do a report-only performance hunt on this codebase. If you do not have profiler or runtime data, say that clearly and tell me the smallest measurements to add first.
- Primary boundary: The repo has code and configs, but live profiler data, traces, or benchmark output may be missing.
- Evidence reviewed: `Code`, `Config files`, `Partial or missing performance artifacts`

## Draft swe:performance-hunt Output
1. Available evidence summary: ground this in Code and keep it bounded to the requested surface.
2. Explicit limitations: ground this in Config files and keep it bounded to the requested surface.
3. Ranked hotspots or clean report: ground this in Partial or missing performance artifacts and keep it bounded to the requested surface.
4. Measurement plan: ground this in Code and keep it bounded to the requested surface.

## Unknowns Or Residual Risk
- The response should extract useful repo-grounded hotspots without overstating confidence.

## Recommended Next Step
- Use this draft as a reviewable starting point, then run only the narrow validation or follow-up work implied by the scoped output.

## Draft swe:performance-hunt Output
1. Bounded first-pass scope: ground this in Repo layout and keep it bounded to the requested surface.
2. Target metric: ground this in Performance-sensitive code paths and keep it bounded to the requested surface.
3. Ranked first-pass backlog: ground this in Build or benchmark configuration and keep it bounded to the requested surface.
4. Remaining backlog: ground this in Recent change history and keep it bounded to the requested surface.

## Unknowns Or Residual Risk
- The best response should leave a bounded follow-up backlog instead of pretending the whole app can be optimized at once.

## Recommended Next Step
- Use this draft as a reviewable starting point, then run only the narrow validation or follow-up work implied by the scoped output.

- Reviewed first: `Repo layout`, `Performance-sensitive code paths`, `Build or benchmark configuration`, `Recent change history`

## swe:performance-hunt Draft
1. Bounded first-pass scope: tie this to Repo layout, then stop at the requested boundary.
2. Target metric: tie this to Performance-sensitive code paths, then stop at the requested boundary.
3. Ranked first-pass backlog: tie this to Build or benchmark configuration, then stop at the requested boundary.
4. Remaining backlog: tie this to Recent change history, then stop at the requested boundary.

## Labeled Unknowns
- The request is intentionally broad and should be narrowed to one performance surface and one target metric for a first pass.
- The best response should leave a bounded follow-up backlog instead of pretending the whole app can be optimized at once.

## Smallest Next Step
- Use this draft as the narrowest reviewable starting point, then validate only the touched surface.
